indicator_text: At least some lobbying data available (in any format) either by request (email, telephone, fax, etc.) or anonymously (web-based).
The state has a dedicated website for lobbying information.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_accessibility_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §IV Q2. Likely obsolete for 2026 — flag for Phase 3.

indicator_text: How easily a state's lobbying website can be found.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_accessibility_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §IV Q3 (footnote 92 in source). PRI does not define "easily" — flag for Phase 3.

indicator_text: At least some current lobbying data/information available on the website for the most recent year.
Historical lobbying information is available, downloadable, or at least viewable on the state's website.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_accessibility_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §IV Q5. "Historical" horizon not specified — flag for Phase 3.

indicator_text: How easily users can download lobbying data and information and analyze it, and specifically, whether users are able to download information in an electronic format that is immediately useable for analysis, such as Excel or SPSS.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_accessibility_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §IV Q6. 2010 treated CSV/Excel as gold standard; 2026 should reward bulk download / API access — flag for Phase 3.

indicator_text: Can users specify data searches on the website by Principal (city; authority hiring; employer; corporation; etc.).
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_accessibility_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §IV Q7. Q7 = 15 sub-criteria a–o; original PRI scoring weighted each at 1 point (sub-component max 15).

indicator_text: Can users specify data searches on the website by Principal's location (specific address; city).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Lobbyist name.
Can users specify data searches on the website by Lobbyist location (specific address; city).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Specific date.
Can users specify data searches on the website by Specific time period (normally quarter but it depends on how often the law requires data to be disclosed).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Total expenditures.
Can users specify data searches on the website by Compensation spending (portion of total expenditures for which the lobbyist is paid back).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Miscellaneous expenses (non-compensation).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Sources of funding (emphasis on public/taxpayer-funded).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Subject of lobbying (or item of legislation).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Designated entities assigned to lobbyist (group/congressman the lobbyist is working for).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Legal status of the principal (government; non-profit; for-profit; etc.).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Sector (transport; energy; banking; education; social services; etc.).
Can users specify data searches on the website by Sub-sector (K–12; secondary; vocational; etc.).
Users can simultaneously sort lobbying data by multiple criteria.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_accessibility_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §IV Q8. Original PRI raw 0–15 then divided by 15 to a 0–1 sub-score; the 0–15 rubric is not specified in the paper — flag for Phase 3.

indicator_text: Lobbyists.
section_or_category: A. Who is required to register
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.A. Per paper: all 50 states require lobbyists to register.

notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.A. Per paper: 17 states required as of 2010.

notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.A. Per paper: 24 states required as of 2010.

indicator_text: Is there an exemption for government agencies and/or public officials acting within their official capacity?
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.B. Footnote 85 in source. Note: "exemption exists" reduces transparency; whether this counts positively or negatively in the score is not stated explicitly — flag for Phase 3.

indicator_text: Are government agencies relieved and/or exempted (partially) from the regulations imposed on non-government organizations and individuals?
Are government agencies subject to the same disclosure requirements as lobbyists?
Are government agencies subject to the same disclosure requirements as principals?
Does the law include a definition of "public entity"?
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.C. Gate question — Table 5 reports C as 0/1 only, suggesting the published score reflected only this question. Sub-criteria C1–C3 below are described in the paper but their scoring contribution is not specified — flag for Phase 3.

indicator_text: Definition relies on Ownership.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.C. Sub-criterion of C0; only meaningful if C0=1.

indicator_text: Definition relies on Structure or composition of revenue.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.C. Sub-criterion of C0; only meaningful if C0=1.

indicator_text: Definition relies on Public charter or special government protection.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.C. Sub-criterion of C0; only meaningful if C0=1.

indicator_text: Does the law include a materiality test (ability to exempt) for coverage in disclosure regulations?
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.D. Gate question — Table 5 reports D as 0/1 only.

indicator_text: Financial threshold exists: if expenses are less than some dollar threshold the entity is exempted from filing disclosure.
Dollar threshold value (USD) for financial-threshold exemption.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.D. Capture raw value; null if no threshold.

indicator_text: Time threshold exists: if amount of time devoted to lobbying is less than a threshold percentage of an individual's compensated time the individual or entity is exempted from filing disclosure.
Percentage threshold value for time-threshold exemption.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.D. Capture raw value; null if no threshold.

indicator_text: Are principals required to disclose?
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.E1. Gate question for the rest of E1.

indicator_text: Are principals required to disclose their address and phone number?
Are principals required to disclose the names of all the lobbyists representing them?
Are principals required to disclose the address and phone number of contracted lobbyists?
Are principals required to disclose the nature of the business (public or private)?
Required component of disclosure report: Direct lobbying costs (compensation).
Required component of disclosure report: Indirect lobbying costs (non-compensation).
Required component of disclosure report: Other costs such as gifts, entertainment, transportation, and lodging.
Is the information disclosed in an itemized format (as opposed to an aggregated or lump-sum amount)?
Are principals required to disclose information on the issue lobbied by the general issues lobbied?
Are principals required to disclose information on the issue lobbied by the specific bill number or legislation ID?
Reporting frequency option: Monthly.
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.E1.h. Capture each frequency option as a separate item; downstream scoring per PRI footnote 89 awards "1 point for reporting … less than three times a year" (paper wording is ambiguous) — defer scoring rule to Phase 3.

indicator_text: Reporting frequency option: Quarterly.
Reporting frequency option: Tri-annually (linked with legislative calendar).
Reporting frequency option: Semi-annually.
Reporting frequency option: Annually.
Reporting frequency option: Other (free-text).
Are principals required to disclose contacts?
Are principals required to disclose major financial contributors?
notes: Source: docs/historical/pri-2026-rescore/results/pri_2010_disclosure_law_rubric.csv. PRI 2010 §III.E1.j. Per paper §III.E intro: scored independently of the rest of E (this is "question J" referenced in the text).
